skill.name: repairing-signal-tower
skill.description: Guide a science fiction roleplay chapter where Stella repairs a signal tower and decides what to tell Earth.
skill.summary: Runs a bilingual survival story scene about repairing a signal tower on Gaia.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and entropy patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown story blocks and bilingual narrative text, with no executable code, network activity, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Story Blocks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fenced blocks that contain character dialogue and scene directions. They are not executable commands and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, and each reported command location is a fenced narrative excerpt. No code runner, shell invocation, script file, or command arguments are present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hit points to the YAML description line. The line only describes a signal tower story premise and contains no cryptographic algorithm or security protocol.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text in Chinese about repairing or building a signal transmitter. There is no hashing, encryption, key handling, or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are story references to scientists, planetary water, and science writing guidance. They do not collect host, network, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are narrative content and writing guidance. They contain no commands, APIs, or instructions to inspect the user's system.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy from Bilingual Narrative
low_findings.description: The file mixes Chinese and English story text, which can raise entropy heuristics. Manual review found readable Markdown content and no encoded payload or binary data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is human-readable Markdown with front matter and story sections. No obfuscated strings, decoder logic, or hidden payloads were found.

content.value_statement: Long survival stories can lose focus during technical chapters. This skill structures a signal tower repair scene with emotion, engineering, and ethical choices.
actual_capabilities: Provides a staged outline for a signal tower repair chapter.
Guides Stella Chen's voice, goals, and emotional conflict.
Includes scene beats for planning, resource gathering, construction, transmission, and aftermath.
Offers branching endings for Earth contact, alien response, or failure.
Supports bilingual Chinese and English narration.
limitations: It is a writing guide, not a real engineering manual.
It does not run commands or build communication hardware.
It assumes prior story context about Stella and Gaia.
It does not include automated continuity checks across other chapters.

use_cases.title: Run a Survival Roleplay Session
use_cases.description: Use the staged beats to guide a player through repairing a long-range transmitter.

use_cases.title: Draft a Science Fiction Chapter
use_cases.description: Turn the outline into a chapter about improvised engineering and homesickness.

use_cases.title: Design Branching Story Choices
use_cases.description: Use the ending routes to plan consequences for rescue, alien contact, or failure.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Repair Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for opening the chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the repairing-signal-tower skill. Start the chapter with Stella inspecting the damaged communication panel and asking for my advice.

prompt_templates.title: Play Through Construction
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a focused scene.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the repairing-signal-tower skill. Run the resource gathering and tower construction stages with believable engineering details and player choices.

prompt_templates.title: Explore the Ethical Choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the repairing-signal-tower skill. Focus on Stella deciding whether to reveal Gaia's ecosystem and ruins in her message to Earth.

prompt_templates.title: Branch the Ending
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the repairing-signal-tower skill. Present three possible outcomes: Earth replies, an unknown Gaia signal replies, or the transmission fails but Stella grows.

output_examples.input: Start the signal tower chapter and ask me whether Stella should attempt the repair.
output_examples.output: Stella opens the damaged panel, lists the missing components, and explains why the hill could extend the signal. She asks whether the risk is worth taking.

output_examples.input: Continue after the tower starts transmitting.
output_examples.output: The scene shifts to the control panel. Stella records a concise survival message, then pauses over whether to reveal Gaia's secrets.

output_examples.input: Give me a mysterious response instead of Earth contact.
output_examples.output: After days of silence, Stella receives a patterned signal from an unexplored region of Gaia. The moment changes the story from rescue to first contact.

best_practices: Keep Stella's technical problem solving tied to her homesickness.
Let the player influence message content and risk tolerance.
Use the branch endings to preserve future story options.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the fictional tower design as real engineering advice.
Do not skip the ethical dilemma about what Earth should know.
Do not resolve rescue too quickly if the campaign needs survival tension.

faq.question: What kind of skill is this?
faq.answer: It is a narrative roleplay skill for a science fiction survival chapter.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The Markdown code fences contain story dialogue and scene beats only.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it only for Chinese readers?
faq.answer: No. It uses mixed Chinese and English, and it can guide bilingual scenes.

faq.question: Can it create alternate endings?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes routes for Earth contact, mysterious alien response, and failed transmission.

faq.question: Does it require earlier chapters?
faq.answer: It works best with prior context about Stella, Gaia, the crash, and the survival timeline.
